content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 22, 1995

Robert W. Ford, Chair
Political Action Committee of CDFEA
924 Enterprise Drive
Sacramento, California 95825

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-95-067

Dear Mr. Ford:

You have requested advice regarding the "personal use" provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

QUESTIONS

1) May the funds from a statewide general purpose PAC be used to support the election of candidates campaigning for higher office in union organizations?

2) If PAC funds may not be used for the above purpose, may general membership dues be utilized?

CONCLUSIONS

1) Under the Act, the union's PAC funds may not be used to support the election of candidates to office within the union organization.

2) The expenditure of the union's general membership dues is not under the jurisdiction of the Act.

FACTS

California Department of Forestry Employees Association ("CDFEA") is an affiliate of a statewide union (California Professional Firefighters). It is also Local 2881 of the International Association of Firefighters ("IAFF") and pays membership dues to both organizations.

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections 18000-18954.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

File No. A-95-067
Page 2


The CDPEA would like to support individuals from its local
union (Local 2881) and other IAFF locals in California who are
campaigning for offices in the California Professional
Firefighters and the IAFF. You would like to know if PAC funds
may be used to support the campaigns of these candidates.

ANALYSIS

The general rule is that an expenditure of campaign funds by
a committee not controlled by a candidate must be reasonably
related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose of
the committee. (Section 89512.5(a).)

PAC funds may be expended to support a candidate or
candidates, but only as they are defined by the Act. Section
82007 defines a "candidate" as an individual who is listed on the
ballot or who is seeking elective office. The Act defines
"elective office" as any state, regional, county, municipal,
district or judicial office which is filled at an election.

The term "candidate" does not refer to an individual who is
seeking office within a union organization. Therefore, under the
Act, the unions' PAC funds may not be used to support the election
of candidates to office within the union organization. Such an
expenditure would not qualify as a political, legislative or
governmental purpose of the committee.

You have also inquired if general membership dues could be
utilized to support the candidates for office within the union
organization. The expenditure of the union's membership dues is
not under the jurisdiction of the Political Reform Act and we are
not able to answer this question. Please consult with the union's
legal counsel regarding this matter.

I trust this answers your questions.

Sincerely,


Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Jill Stecher
Counsel, Legal Division
